evidence: 4063h: LXI HL,$6000; 4066h: LDAX (HL+A). The 6000h block is a 256-byte identity table; printable bytes pass through unchanged unless modified by international character substitution at 1464h.
notes: The remap table at 6000h is the first step after raw byte classification. Characters in the $20-$AF range take the normal CG path; $B0-$DF and $E0-$EF take extended paths with different style state sources.

evidence: 4081h: B = remapped char. 4084h: VV21 = VV:A2. 4088h: VV22 = VV:CA. 408Ch: VV1F = VV:CB. Then sets VV22 bits 2/1, checks VV:A3 bit 7 for VV22 bit 0, and calls 2824h for extended-range classification.
notes: VV:A2/VV:CA/VV:CB are the persistent character/font/style state set by font commands (ESC k, ESC x, ESC !, etc.). VV21/VV22/VV1F are the per-character working copies.

evidence: 4124h: B = char. VV21 = VV:2D. VV22 = (VV22 & 08h) | VV:2E. VV1F = VV:2F. If VV21 bit 7 set, calls 1464h for international substitution.
notes: The $B0-$EF extended range uses VV:2D/VV:2E/VV:2F as style sources instead of VV:A2/VV:CA/VV:CB. VV22 bit 3 (italic) is preserved from the main state.

evidence: 4012h: B = A (save char). 4013h: CALT ($0096) → 1845h. If CALT returns nonzero, calls 4142h to check rendering conditions, then 4173h for line-buffer fit. At 4026h: stores HL to EE78 and calls CALT ($0098) → 1896h for line advance.
notes: Printable bytes reach this path because 4038h returns via RETS, skipping the JMP $6944 command dispatch at 400Fh.

evidence: 1B19h: STAW VV:A0. Called from the CALT ($0096) path at 1845h → 184Ah → 1B19h with the character code in A. Also called from 1A8Ah (alternate render path) and 40FBh (DEL/7F special case).
notes: VV:A0 is the character code used by all downstream CG access. After storing VV:A0, the code at 1B1Bh clears VV:9F and proceeds to CG bank selection.

evidence: 1B1Fh: A = VV:26. 1B21h: ONI A,$80 — if VV:26 bit 7 set, takes normal CG path at 1B2Eh. If clear, takes user-defined character path: C = char code, DE = $8900, F002 = $C0 (external RAM), jumps to 1B4Bh.
notes: VV:26 bit 7 distinguishes CG ROM characters from user-defined/downloaded characters stored in external RAM at 8900h.

evidence: 1774h: A = VV:26 & 7F, DE = A as 16-bit. Checks VV:04 bits to select font bank. VV:04 bit 7 → use VV:05 instead. VV:04 bits 6/5 select font family; VV:04 bit 4 selects a sub-variant. 17F5h: writes A to F002 via CALT ($00B4), then writes B to $8000 as sub-page selector.
notes: F002 bank values observed: $80, $81, $82, $00 depending on VV:04/VV:05 font configuration. VV:A7 is set to $80 or $00 to track the access mode. After the F002+$8000 write, the code reads back from $8000 to validate the font's presence.

evidence: 17FFh: reads byte from (HL+) at $8000. Checks for $00 (empty) or $FF (missing) → RETS if so. Otherwise: C = byte & C0h (top 2 bits), A = byte & 3Fh (count/range). Computes record offset: base + adjusted_count * 12 or 15 (MUL result) + 3. Stores F002 to VV:A8 before returning.
notes: The font header byte at $8000 encodes the character range and format. The top 2 bits select the glyph record size (12 or 15 bytes per character). The low 6 bits are a count/range adjustment.

evidence: 1B4Bh: EA = DE (record base from 173Dh). 1B4Ch-1B54h: EA += C * 5 (five EADD EA,C), with conditional 6th add based on VV:26/VV:A7. Then: byte 0 → EF97 (start column offset as word). Byte 1 → VV99 (active width). Byte 2 → sign-extended into HL (advance/kerning). These metrics position the glyph within the print line.
notes: Record size is 5 bytes when VV:A7 bit 6 is clear, 6 bytes when set. This matches the CG having different record formats for different font types (e.g., fixed-width vs proportional).

evidence: 1CF2h: saves F002 to B. Checks VV28 bit 4: if set → HL=$8000, if clear → HL=$8600. Writes F002=$4F via CALT ($00B4). Computes EA = HL + VV:A0 * 6 (six EADD). Skips byte 0, reads bytes 1 and 2 into EF99 and EF9B. Restores F002 from B.
notes: This second CG read uses a fixed F002=$4F bank and a uniform 6-byte record at the $8000h or $8600h window. It is reached when VV27 bit 2 is clear (at 1CEDh). The 6-byte records at this bank likely contain LQ-mode metrics or glyph pointers separate from the Draft-mode data.

evidence: No F002 writes exist in the 43DDh-4C36h traced region. The bitmap expansion engine reads glyph data from the CG window at 8000h+ using the F002 bank value set earlier by 1774h/17F5h. VV:A8 stores the saved F002 value for later restoration.
notes: The CG bank is set once before entering the expansion engine and remains active throughout. This means glyph bitmap data and metrics coexist in the same banked page selected by the font/quality configuration.

evidence: Decoded all 32 CALT vectors from raw ROM bytes. Key render-path vectors: ($0080)→$0A0B, ($0092)→$14C6 (font reconfig), ($0096)→$1845 (printable entry), ($0098)→$1896 (line advance), ($009A)→$1A4F, ($009C)→$1995, ($00AC)→$0ADF (word decrement), ($00B4)→$0B23 (F002 write).
notes: CALT ($00B4) is the F002 bank-write helper used throughout the CG path. The CALT table is at fixed ROM addresses with 2-byte little-endian target vectors.
